serving.natural_language_portion: One standard quinoa tofu patty burger comprising a handmade patty, burger bun, and typical garnishes, estimated at approximately 280 g per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Tofu, raw (FDC ID 172475); Quinoa, cooked (FDC ID 168917); Hamburger bun, plain (FDC ID 174927); Lettuce, tomato, onion, condiment estimates
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for quinoa tofu patty burger exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition: approximately 35% tofu patty (tofu + quinoa blend), 40% burger bun, 15% vegetables, 10% condiments/sauce. Per-100g values derived from this composite estimate. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for tofu-based and quinoa-based foods
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a quinoa-tofu patty burger. GI estimated at 48 based on weighted analogous components: quinoa cooked GI ~53 (University of Sydney GI Database), tofu GI ~15 (University of Sydney GI Database), white burger bun GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by carbohydrate contribution per serving: bun (~55% of carbs, GI 70), quinoa (~35% of carbs, GI 53), tofu (~10% of carbs, GI 15). Weighted GI = (0.55×70)+(0.35×53)+(0.10×15) = 38.5+18.55+1.5 ≈ 58.6 for bun-dominant scenario; however, with vegetable and fibre buffering in the full burger assembly, a composite GI of approximately 48 is defensible. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (48 × 79.8) / 100 ≈ 38.3, rounded to 38.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full serving (~280 g) contributes approximately 868 mg sodium, representing 38% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful of the sodium contribution from condiments and sauces in this burger and consider requesting reduced-sauce options to keep sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~38 per serving) driven primarily by the burger bun; quinoa and tofu components contribute low-GI carbohydrates and protein that moderate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a wholegrain or lettuce-wrap alternative to the standard bun to reduce the glycemic load, and pair this burger with non-starchy vegetables to further blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.0 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and plant-based protein from tofu and quinoa support a heart-healthy dietary pattern; polyunsaturated fat content is favourable.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This plant-based burger is a heart-friendly alternative to meat-based burgers due to its low saturated fat and zero cholesterol content; however, sodium from condiments should be monitored in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Quinoa Tofu Patty Burger is a handmade plant-based burger served at Flavours by Saute in Singapore. The patty is crafted from a blend of cooked quinoa and firm tofu, shaped by hand and pan-fried or grilled to achieve a lightly crisp exterior with a tender, protein-rich interior. Quinoa contributes a nutty, slightly earthy flavour and provides all nine essential amino acids, making this burger a complete-protein vegetarian option. Tofu adds a soft, creamy texture and is a rich source of plant-based calcium and iron. The assembled burger is served in a standard bun with lettuce, tomato, onion, and condiments. Nutritionally, the burger provides approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with 8.5 g protein, 5.8 g fat (predominantly unsaturated), and 28.5 g carbohydrate. Dietary fibre at 2.8 g per 100 g supports satiety and glycaemic moderation. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), making it a more suitable burger option for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is moderate at 310 mg per 100 g, primarily from condiments and seasoning. This burger is suitable for vegetarians, is dairy-free, and is halal-certified at the restaurant. It is not suitable for those with soy or gluten allergies. Clinically, the low saturated fat and plant-based protein profile make this a cardiovascular-friendly choice, while the moderate glycemic load warrants portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for a quinoa tofu patty burger exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional nutrition database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for tofu, quinoa, and burger bun using a weighted composite approach. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation methodology and absence of a direct laboratory-analysed entry for this specific dish.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for tofu (FDC 172475), cooked quinoa (FDC 168917), and plain hamburger bun (FDC 174927), weighted by approximate ingredient proportions in a standard 280 g serving. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA component data and are not directly measured for this dish. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based burger with no animal-derived ingredients contributing B12; confirmed zero by nature of ingredients (naturally zero for plant-based foods). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as tofu and quinoa contain negligible vitamin D unless fortified; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data (naturally zero for unfortified plant-based foods). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as tofu and quinoa are plant-based and contain no dietary cholesterol (naturally zero). GI estimated at 48 using weighted analogous component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; see gi_source for full derivation. Serving size of 280 g is an estimate for a standard assembled burger; actual restaurant serving may vary. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style 'BG3-Handmade Quinoa Tofu Patty Burger' to the canonical 'Quinoa Tofu Patty Burger'. Cuisine classified as Global per input; region_of_origin set to Singapore as the dish is served exclusively at a Singapore restaurant. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 rules. is_vegan set to false as condiments and sauces at the restaurant may contain non-vegan ingredients; is_vegetarian set to true as the core ingredients are plant-based.
